name: Mariachi Beefballs and Rice
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Monterey Jack cheese, cut in strips
4 oz Green chillies, drained and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, lightly combine ground beef with corn chips, milk, egg, and 2 teaspoons salt. Cover and refrigerate for 1 hour.
2. Shape the mixture into 15 meatballs using about 2 rounded tablespoons of meat mixture for each.
3. Lightly roll each meatball in 2 tablespoons flour, coating completely.
4. In a large frying pan, cook the meatballs in hot butter, half at a time, stirring until evenly browned. Remove meatballs from the pan as they are browned.
5. In the same frying pan, cook onion and garlic about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
6. In a small bowl, combine remaining 1 tablespoon flour and 1 teaspoon salt. Stir into onions.
7. Add tomatoes, green chilies, and olives. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly; reduce heat, cover tightly, and cook slowly for 30 minutes.
8. Add meatballs to the tomato mixture, cover tightly, and cook slowly for 20 minutes.
9. Uncover and continue cooking slowly for an additional 10 minutes.
10. Serve meatballs and sauce over hot Mexican Rice.
Mexican Rice:
1. Combine sour cream, chopped green chilies, and salt in a bowl.
2. In a 13 x 9-inch baking pan, layer 1 cup cooked rice, half of the sour cream-chili mixture, and half of the cheese strips.
3. Repeat layers with remaining ingredients: 1 cup of rice, remaining sour cream-chili mixture, and cheese strips.
4. Bake in a moderate oven (350°F) for 25 minutes.
5. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese and top with meatballs and sauce. Continue baking for an additional 5 minutes or until the cheese melts.
